documents.text: athena 2/17/2026 2:17:52 pm EST 893cc94a-9cd2-4cc2-b325-9583ca6ca5bf Page: 01 / 14 CONFIDENTIAL Fax FROM: JASON SHERMAN, MD 8952 E Desert Cove Avenue, SCOTTSDALE, AZ 85260-6740 Phone: (623) 537-5600 Fax: (866) 939-2673 TO: STAR HOME HEALTH CARE 2415 E CAMELBACK RD, PHOENIX, AZ 85016 Phone: (480) 931-6009 Fax: (480) 956-0654 If you would like to receive this document through Direct Message, please send an email to athenahealth at "DirectMessaging@athenahealth.com" with only your Direct Address. Do not include any other information in your email, as any messages containing additional details (including patient information) will be deleted immediately. This process enables athenahealth to securely share future documents electronically via Direct Message. This fax may contain sensitive and confidential personal health information that is being sent for the sole use of the intended recipient. Unintended recipients are directed to securely destroy any materials received. You are hereby notified that the unauthorized disclosure or other unlawful use of this fax or any personal health information is prohibited. To the extent patient information contained in this fax is subject to 42 CFR Part 2, this regulation prohibits unauthorized disclosure of these records. If you received this fax in error, please visit www.athenahealth.com/NotMyFax to notify the sender and confirm that the information will be destroyed. If you do not have internet access, please call 1-888-482-8436 to notify the sender and confirm that the information will be destroyed. Thank you for your attention and cooperation. Medications: jsherman56 Not 02/17/2026 -Continue oxycodone 5mg tid. available 13:47:12 -Rx for narcan filled on 6/4/24 -gabapentin did not help her upper limb paresthesias; consider lyrica 2. Injections: -Will repeat a L5-S1 L ESI to target her L4-5 central stenosis. The procedure recommended, with its inherent risks and realistic benefits, was discussed in detail including but not limited to death, pain, bleeding, Infection, headache, nerve Injury (elther mlld and translent or severe such as spinal cord injury with secondary paresis), reaction to medications including raised blood glucose levels, breathing difficulties, or cardiac difficulties. The patient was counseled on conscious sedation in the event that this is used during the procedure. -Continue use of her SCS unit. She was using it at her previous residence, but her aid is not sure if she has the remote at her new residence. 3. Imaging: -MRI lumbar spine reviewed and scanned into her chart. 4. Physical Therapy: -Start home PT. 5. Consultation: -She is not interested in consulting with a spine surgeon. -She was seen by Dr. Sumner and did PT. She is not interested in surgery for this. Patient Instructions Encounter Encounter Patient Instructions Last OrganizationLast Date Id Modified By Details Modified Time 02/17/2026 14317530 learning about healthy weight Jason Not 02/17/2026 Sherman, available 13:20:06 MD Opiate agreement/ Medication Management Agreement reviewed/signed in the office. Agreement has been jsherman56 Not 02/13/2026 scanned into the chart. available 10:59:41 Discussed the risks of opioid medication for pain management. These risks include: abnormal thinking, constipation, addiction, medication tolerance, nausea, physical dependence, opioid-induced hyperalgesia (increase in pain from opioid medications), respiratory depression, sedation and substance abuse. The patient has been warned to keep medication in a safe place as lost or stolen medication will not be replaced. They have also been told not to drink alcohol or illicit drugs and to avoid taking other central nervous system depressant such as benzodiazepine due to the synergistic effect which can substantially increase the risks of side effects. The patient was specifically told not to drive or operate heavy machinery as this medication may adversely affect their driving. The patient has been informed that The CORE Institute conforms to all the recommendations of the Arizona Medical Board. The AZ State Board of Pharmacy prescription monitoring web site was reviewed today and reveals that the patient is adherent and does not reveal any suspicious activity to suggest abuse or diversion. Urine drug screens (UDS) will be periodically obtained to monitor for compliance, as per The CORE Institute policy. UDS done on 3/7/23 was consistent for prescribed opiates and EtS. She states that she only takes this as needed and if she does not use this when she uses alcohol. An ODS was done on 1/30/24, 12/30/24, 3/31/25 were consistent for her prescribed medications. She is low risk for opiod abuse per ORT. Hand dominance: right Location: lower lumbar Radiation: radiation to right leg ( hip Pain level: lumbar spine 0/10; right leg /10 right hip - 0) Back pain timing: intermittent Leg pain timing: constant Quality: dull; aching Modifying factors: Lwisting worsens; sitting worsens; standing worsens ( up straight ); walking - worsens; lying flat - improves: all ROM and al rest Associated symptoms: no numbness; no tingling; no headache; no sleep interruption; Bilateral hands Prior treatments: injection some improvement ( 8/6/25 L5-S1 interlaminar epidural steroid injection ): physical therapy some improvement Ms. Haug returns to clinic for ongoing management of her back pain and hand paresthesias. She does have some aching pain in the low back that only mildly radiates into the posterior legs. This is worse with walking and standing. Better with laying down and sitting. She also notes numbness/tingling and weakness in both hands. She had an EMG/NCS that showed severe CTS. She was seen by Dr. Sumner who sent her to PT which has not helped. She does not want to have surgery. She is taking oxycodone 5mg tid prn. This allows her to function and do her ADLs.
